revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:アスリートの性的画像問題]]
'''Athlete Sexual Imagery Problem''' (アスリートの性的画像問題) refers to the social issue in which {{ill|Athlete|en|Athlete|qid=Q2066131|lt=Athlete|1=Athlete}}s competing are secretly filmed or photographed for sexual purposes and the images or videos are disseminated without consent<ref>{{Cite news |url = https://www.tokyo-np.co.jp/article/62577 |title = 〈女性アスリートの性的画像問題〉被害選手に聞く「相談窓口作って」 |date = 2020-10-18 |newspaper= 東京新聞 |accessdate = 2021-07-06|qq=}}</ref><ref name="tokyo103479">{{cite news |url = https://www.tokyo-np.co.jp/article/103479 |title = 女性アスリートの性的画像拡散 選手自身が被害訴え 「盗撮罪」を求める声も |date = 2021-05-11 |author=佐藤大 |newspaper= 東京新聞 |accessdate = 2021-07-06|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Cite news |url = https://web.archive.org/web/20210613031631/https://www.sanspo.com/sports/news/20210511/spo21051119180005-n1.html |title = 女性アスリート性的画像問題「立件はいい前例」被害の深刻さ訴える声も |date = 2021-05-11 |newspaper= サンスポ |accessdate = 2021-07-06|qq=}}</ref><ref name="47news_224896">{{Cite news |url = https://nordot.app/782502303126224896 |title = 隠し撮り画像流出、チアリーダー「身の危険感じた」 |date = 2021-07-02 |publisher = 全国新聞ネット |accessdate = 2021-07-06|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|和書|url = https://www.yomiuri.co.jp/national/20210705-OYT1T50273/ |title = 女性アスリート画像の性的悪用被害300件、JOCが警視庁に新たに情報提供 |date = 2021-07-05 |publisher = 全国新聞ネット |accessdate = 2021-07-06|qq=}}</ref>. This phenomenon is also known as '''Athlete Voyeurism''' (アスリート盗撮)<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url = https://web.archive.org/web/20210528114704/https://www3.nhk.or.jp/news/html/20210528/k10013051801000.html |title = ただ、走りたかっただけなのに…アスリート盗撮被害の実態 |date = 2021-05-28 |publisher = NHK |accessdate = 2023-08-22|qq=}}</ref>. == Explanation == Female {{ill|Athlete|en|Athlete|qid=Q2066131|lt=Athlete|1=Athlete}}s being filmed for sexual purposes and the subsequent spread of those images and videos have long been recognized as a serious problem<ref name="tokyo103479" />. The issue came to light in 2020 when a current women's {{ill|Sport of athletics|en|Sport of athletics|qid=Q542|lt=Athletics|1=Sport of athletics}} athlete raised a complaint to the Athlete Committee, prompting formal investigations and countermeasures. In response to the increasing number of reported cases, seven related organizations—including the {{ill|Japanese Olympic Committee|en|Japanese Olympic Committee|qid=Q971827|lt=Japanese Olympic Committee|1=Japanese Olympic Committee}} (JOC) and the {{ill|Japan Sports Association|de|Japanische Sportvereinigung|ja|日本スポーツ協会|zh|日本体育协会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan Sports Association|1=Japan Sport Association|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11507232}}—issued a joint statement in November 2020 condemning these acts as "despicable" and labeling them as {{ill|Sexual harassment|en|Sexual harassment|qid=Q751722|lt=Sexual harassment|1=Sexual harassment}}<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url = https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO66182970T11C20A1000000/ |title = JOCなど、性的画像の被害撲滅へ共同声明 |date = 2020-11-13 |publisher = 日本経済新聞 |accessdate = 2021-07-06|qq=}}</ref><ref name="tokyo103479" />. The JOC also established an official reporting hotline on its website<ref>[https://www.joc.or.jp/about/savesport/ アスリートへの写真・動画による性的ハラスメント防止の取り組みについて - JOC]</ref>, calling for information on on-site voyeurism, misuse of images or videos, and malicious social media posts. These illicit images have even been traded as commodities, with dedicated sales sites operating online<ref name="nikkan20201209">{{Cite news |url = https://www.nikkansports.com/sports/news/202012090000007.html |title = 性的画像を批判受けても投稿 男性の理解不能な主張 |date = 2020-12-09 |author=平山連 |newspaper= 日刊スポーツ |accessdate = 2021-07-06|qq=}}</ref><ref name="47news_224896"/>. {{ill|Meiji University|en|Meiji University|qid=Q46492|lt=Meiji University|1=Meiji University}} professor Osamu Takamine has noted, "While voyeurism against athletes and cheerleaders has existed for decades, it only surfaced publicly in the past two to three years. Sports federations and event organizers have been slow and reluctant to address the issue of sexual victimization."<ref name="huffpostbd9f3a"/> == Victim Statistics == Surveys and reports have documented numerous incidents, including the following examples. Male {{ill|Athlete|en|Athlete|qid=Q2066131|lt=Athlete|1=Athlete}} victims have also been confirmed<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url = https://hochi.news/articles/20201016-OHT1T50156.html |title = 性的画像被害は男性アスリートも JOCが国内競技団体と会議 |date = 2020-10-16 |publisher = スポーツ報知 |accessdate = 2021-07-07|qq=}}</ref>. * The {{ill|Japanese Olympic Committee|en|Japanese Olympic Committee|qid=Q971827|lt=JOC|1=Japanese Olympic Committee}}’s reporting hotline, open since November 2020, had received approximately 1,300 reports by July 2021<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url = https://www.yomiuri.co.jp/national/20210705-OYT1T50273/ |title = 女性アスリート画像の性的悪用被害300件、JOCが警視庁に新たに情報提供 |date = 2020-07-05 |publisher = 読売新聞 |accessdate = 2021-07-06|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|和書|url = https://www.asahi.com/articles/ASP756W83P75UTIL04T.html |title = アスリートの画像悪用巡り JOCと警視庁が対策会議 五輪前に |date = 2020-07-05 |publisher = 読売新聞 |accessdate = 2021-07-06|qq=}}</ref> * The {{ill|Japan Association of Athletics Federations|en|Japan Association of Athletics Federations|qid=Q1505454|lt=Japan Association of Athletics Federations|1=Japan Association of Athletics Federations}}’s 2021 survey found that 20 of 37 member and partner organizations had reported or consulted police in the previous three years<ref name="huffpost3b50c0">{{Cite web|和書|url = https://www.huffingtonpost.jp/entry/story_jp_60a37dcde4b099ba753b50c0 |title = 性的画像「盗撮1人じゃない」陸上界で通報や被害届⇒専門家「取り締まる法律が必要」 |date = 2020-06-03 |publisher = HUFFPOST |accessdate = 2021-07-06|qq=}}</ref> * A {{ill|Kyodo News|en|Kyodo News|qid=Q779730|lt=Kyodo News|1=Kyodo News}} 2021 survey of 15 schools planning {{ill|Cheerleading|en|Cheerleading|qid=Q61391|lt=Cheerleading|1=Cheerleading}} at the {{ill|Spring Koshien|de|Frühling Koshien|ja|第93回選抜高等学校野球大会|zh|春季甲子园|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Koshien|1=93rd Japanese High School Baseball Invitational Tournament|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q108131376}} found that eight schools were aware of voyeurism incidents<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url = https://www.sankei.com/article/20210306-Q6J3XZLPVJOB7KH6FVVLSRYS3U/ |title = チアリーダーも盗撮被害…選抜高校野球、8校把握 応援予定の半数超 |date = 2020-03-06 |publisher = 産経新聞 |accessdate = 2021-07-06|qq=}}</ref> === Voyeurism Victims: Cheerleaders === Incidents targeting {{ill|Cheerleader (disambiguation)|en|Cheerleader (disambiguation)|qid=Q17503371|lt=Cheerleader|1=Cheerleader (disambiguation)}}s have been frequently reported<ref name="huffpostbd9f3a"/>. For example, at {{ill|Meihō High School|de|Meihō-Oberschule|ja|明豊高等学校|zh|明丰高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meihō High School|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 明豊中学校・高等学校|1=Meihō High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11512637}} in {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|en|Ōita Prefecture|qid=Q133924|lt=Oita Prefecture|1=Ōita Prefecture}}, a cheerleader at {{ill|Koshien Stadium|en|Koshien Stadium|qid=Q959811|lt=Hanshin Koshien Stadium|1=Koshien Stadium}} reported a man inserting a {{ill|Smartphone|en|Smartphone|qid=Q22645|lt=Smartphone|1=Smartphone}} under her skirt from the parent seating area<ref name="huffpostbd9f3a"/>. Although the school filed a police report, the perpetrator’s identity remains unknown<ref name="huffpostbd9f3a"/>. The team advisor subsequently redesigned the uniform—introducing short-sleeve shirts with built-in underpants, {{ill|Leggings|en|Leggings|qid=Q660782|lt=Leggings|1=Leggings}} in summer and {{ill|Jeans|en|Jeans|qid=Q83363|lt=Skinny pants|1=Jeans}} in winter—to prevent recurrence<ref name="huffpostbd9f3a"/>. While some parents initially objected, understanding the reality of the incidents and the risk of {{ill|Digital footprint|en|Digital footprint|qid=Q845916|lt=Digital tattoo|1=Digital footprint}} soon led to acceptance. The new uniform also offered sun and cold protection, earning favor among team members<ref name="huffpostbd9f3a"/>. At the 2023 {{ill|Japanese High School Baseball Championship|en|Japanese High School Baseball Championship|qid=Q1075405|lt=Summer Koshien|1=Japanese High School Baseball Championship}}, at least four schools replaced skirts with baseball-style half-pants in their cheer uniforms to deter voyeurism<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url = https://www.yomiuri.co.jp/sports/koshien/summer/20230822-OYT1T50157/ |title = チアリーダーの衣装、スカートから野球ユニホーム風ハーフパンツに…盗撮対策で |date = 2022-08-22 |publisher = 読売新聞社 |accessdate = 2022-08-22|qq=}}</ref>. The {{ill|Japan High School Baseball Federation|de|Japanische Oberschüler-Baseball-Föderation|ja|日本高校野球連盟|zh|日本高校棒球联盟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan High School Baseball Federation|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本高等学校野球連盟|1=Japan High School Baseball Federation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q5360559}} has received several reports during tournaments and advises participating schools and parents, but measures for general spectators remain limited<ref name="huffpostbd9f3a"/>. == Reactions == === Media === Media outlets have long used images with sexual intent, raising questions about their reporting practices<ref name="47news_359168">{{Cite news |url = https://nordot.app/783552605300359168 |title = スポーツメディアの容姿報道に疑問 「結局、実力主義ではない」【性的画像問題】 |date = 2021-07-03 |author=品川絵里 |agency=共同通信社 |newspaper= 全国新聞ネット |accessdate = 2021-07-06|qq=}}</ref>. For instance, former {{ill|Artistic gymnastics|en|Artistic gymnastics|qid=Q326827|lt=Gymnastics|1=Artistic gymnastics}} representative {{ill|Rie Tanaka|en|Rie Tanaka|qid=Q237371|lt=Rie Tanaka (gymnast)|1=Rie Tanaka}} revealed that her competition photos had appeared as a sealed magazine section<ref name="47news_359168"/>. Current cheerleaders have also criticized media-crafted "ideal" images that prioritize appearance over performance<ref name="47news_359168"/>. Satoko Itani, associate professor of gender and sports marketing at {{ill|Kansai University|en|Kansai University|qid=Q1149393|lt=Kansai University|1=Kansai University}}, argues that commercial sports marketing focuses on catering to male audiences, often neglecting female fans<ref name="47news_359168"/>. She emphasizes the importance of presenting role models whom young girls can admire as both skilled and inspiring athletes<ref name="47news_359168"/>. === Perpetrator Opinions === Some offenders defend their actions or engage in {{ill|Victim blaming|en|Victim blaming|qid=Q1716206|lt=Victim blaming|1=Victim blaming}}: * Claims of {{ill|Freedom of speech|en|Freedom of speech|qid=Q22692|lt=Freedom of expression|1=Freedom of speech}} assert that photographing athletes is not harassment, arguing that stricter regulations force them to relocate abroad and sell images to fund travel costs<ref name="nikkan20201209"/>. * Former {{ill|Badminton|en|Badminton|qid=Q7291|lt=Badminton|1=Badminton}} representative {{ill|Reiko Shiota|en|Reiko Shiota|qid=Q456066|lt=Reiko Shiota|1=Reiko Shiota}} was told, "You're responsible because of the way you dress."<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url = https://times.abema.tv/articles/-/8655330 |title = 「そういう格好をしてるからだと言われた」…潮田玲子、石黒由美子、宇垣美里が語る、女性アスリートとメディア、ファンの関係性 |date = 2021-04-22 |publisher = ABEMA TIMES |accessdate = 2021-07-06|qq=}}</ref>. == Cases Outside Japan == In countries such as {{ill|South Korea|en|South Korea|qid=Q884|lt=South Korea|1=South Korea}} and {{ill|France|en|France|qid=Q142|lt=France|1=France}}, filming and distribution for sexual purposes are legally prohibited<ref name="47news_360448">{{Cite news |url = https://nordot.app/768304278528360448 |title = 性的画像対策、東京五輪にも波及の背景 警視庁初摘発、欧州でも選手ら抗議の動き |date = 2021-05-23 |author=田村崇仁 |agency=共同通信社 |newspaper= 全国新聞ネット |accessdate = 2021-07-06|qq=}}</ref>. At the 2021 {{ill|European Artistic Gymnastics Championships|en|European Artistic Gymnastics Championships|qid=Q1468088|lt=European Artistic Gymnastics Championships|1=European Artistic Gymnastics Championships}} in {{ill|Switzerland|en|Switzerland|qid=Q39|lt=Switzerland|1=Switzerland}}, German women wore "bodysuits" instead of leotards to protest sexual image abuses<ref>{{Cite news |url = https://www.asahi.com/articles/GCO2021042601002227.html |title = 体操女子でボディースーツ 独選手、性的画像に抗議 |date = 2021-04-26 |newspaper= 朝日新聞 |accessdate = 2021-07-06|qq=}}</ref>. The {{ill|International Handball Federation|en|International Handball Federation|qid=Q651333|lt=International Handball Federation|1=International Handball Federation}} faced global criticism for mandating high-exposure bikinis for women in {{ill|Beach handball|en|Beach handball|qid=Q535116|lt=Beach Handball|1=Beach handball}} and fining violators<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=ビーチハンド選手のビキニ拒否問題、ノルウェー側が処分を批判|url=https://www.afpbb.com/articles/-/3357683|website=www.afpbb.com|accessdate=2021-08-02|language=ja|qq=}}</ref>. == Legal Measures == Until the creation of {{ill|Sexual Dignity Filming Crime|de|Sexuelles-Bildaufnahmeverbrechen|ja|性的姿態撮影罪|zh|性姿态拍摄罪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sexual Dignity Filming Crime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 性的な姿態を撮影する行為等の処罰及び押収物に記録された性的な姿態の影像に係る電磁的記録の消去等に関する法律|1=Sexual Dignity Filming Crime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q120759222}} in 2023, Japan lacked a specific criminal statute for {{ill|Secret photography|en|Secret photography|qid=Q840241|lt=Voyeurism|1=Secret photography}}. Cases were prosecuted under prefectural {{ill|Anti-Nuisance Ordinance|de|Belästigungsverordnung|ja|迷惑防止条例|zh|防扰令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anti-Nuisance Ordinance|1=Anti-Nuisance Ordinance|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11639203}}, {{ill|Defamation|en|Defamation|qid=Q191783|lt=Defamation|1=Defamation}}, or {{ill|Copyright law of Japan|en|Copyright law of Japan|qid=Q5169258|lt=Copyright Act|1=Copyright law of Japan}} violations<ref name="tokyo103479"/><ref name="chiba803519">{{Cite web|和書|url = https://www.chibanippo.co.jp/news/national/803519 |title = 女性スポーツ選手の性的動画販売した会社員の男逮捕 名誉毀損容疑で全国初摘発 千葉県警 |date = 2020-06-21 |publisher = 千葉日報 |accessdate = 2021-07-06|qq=}}</ref>. The ambiguity over whether uniformed athletes’ images constitute non-consensual footage and the requirement for victim complaints made prosecutions difficult<ref name="chiba803519"/>. Calls for a specific "Voyeurism Crime" statute have persisted, and the Ministry of Justice’s criminal law panel issued a report in May 2021 advocating stronger voyeurism regulations<ref name="huffpost3b50c0"/>. Lawyer Uetani has also urged mechanisms to allow victims to remove sexual images without criminal proceedings. === Arrest Cases === * May 2021: A 37-year-old man in Kyoto Prefecture was arrested for violating the {{ill|Copyright law of Japan|en|Copyright law of Japan|qid=Q5169258|lt=Copyright Act|1=Copyright law of Japan}}<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url = https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXZQOUE109VN0Q1A510C2000000/ |title = 女性選手画像を無断転用疑い サイト運営者の男逮捕 |date = 2021-05-11 |publisher = 日本経済新聞 |accessdate = 2021-07-06|qq=}}</ref> * June 2021: A 57-year-old man in Chiba Prefecture was arrested for {{ill|Defamation|en|Defamation|qid=Q191783|lt=Defamation|1=Defamation}}<ref name="chiba803519"/> * June 2021: A 36-year-old man in Osaka Prefecture was arrested for {{ill|Defamation|en|Defamation|qid=Q191783|lt=Defamation|1=Defamation}}<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url = https://www.sankei.com/article/20210625-6P66CTOCBBK37PW65O7TYRO54A/ |title = テレビの女性アスリート水着画像、アダルトサイトに掲載で書類送検 |date = 2021-06-25 |publisher = 日本経済新聞 |accessdate = 2021-07-06|qq=}}</ref> == Countermeasures == In July 2022, the {{ill|Kyoto Prefectural Police Headquarters|de|Polizeipräsidium Präfektur Kyoto|ja|京都府警察本部|zh|京都府警察本部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Prefectural Police Headquarters|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 京都府警察|1=Kyoto Prefectural Police|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11375295}} organized a 35-member women’s safety team from {{ill|Kyoto Sangyo University|en|Kyoto Sangyo University|qid=Q2221774|lt=Kyoto Sangyo University|1=Kyoto Sangyo University}}. Ahead of the "55th Kyoto Prefectural High School Youth Athletics Championships" at {{ill|Takebishi Stadium Kyoto|de|Takebishi-Stadion Kyoto|ja|たけびしスタジアム京都|zh|竹锋体育场京都|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takebishi Stadium Kyoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 京都市西京極総合運動公園陸上競技場兼球技場|1=Takebishi Stadium Kyoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q7040605}}, they covered fencing to block telescopic cameras and deployed plainclothes officers around the venue, questioning seven individuals aiming cameras at athletes between August 22–24. In August 2021, officers identified and referred to prosecutors a man photographing female athletes’ lower bodies. Ukyo Police Station’s safety chief remarked on the surprising awareness of voyeurism among those stopped. The team also distributed alert cards for reporting suspicious persons and is considering expansion to other sports<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.yomiuri.co.jp/national/20220830-OYT1T50070/ |title=競技中に狙われる軽装の女子選手…望遠カメラ向けたら職務質問、私服警官が目を光らす |publisher=読売新聞オンライン|date=|qq=}}</ref>. Ahead of the {{ill|Paris Olympics|de|Pariser Olympische Spiele|ja|パリ五輪|zh|巴黎奥运会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Paris Olympics|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to パリオリンピック|1=Paris Olympics|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1179646}}, the Japanese national team unveiled new uniforms made of fabric that appears pixelated under infrared surveillance, preventing clear voyeuristic images<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://www.jiji.com/sp/article?k=2024071400309&g=spo |title=新素材でアスリートを守る 盗撮防止、バレーなどで採用―パリ五輪 |access-date=2024/07/18 |publisher=時事通信社 |date=2024/07/15|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |url=https://the-ans.jp/paris-olympic/441650/ |title=日本の盗撮防止ユニに海外注目 苦節20年、開発までの道のり「パフォーマンスと性的被害抑制の両立を」 |access-date=2024/07/29 |publisher=THE ANSWER |date=2024/07/28|qq=}}</ref>.
==References==
{{translated page|ja|アスリートの性的画像問題|version=104410168|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:アスリートの性的画像問題]] [[de:Problem der sexuellen Bilder von Athleten]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q109594455}} [[Category:Sports scandals]] [[Category:Sexual harassment]] [[Category:Women's sports]] [[Category:Sex crimes]] [[Category:Sex crimes in Japan]] [[Category:Social issues]]
